patches.hypothesis: Refined the patch to handle general cases and avoid overfitting by adding proper null checks, boundary conditions, and edge case handling. Maintained test-passing behavior and fixed the actual root cause.
patches.original_hypothesis: The bug is caused by the method throwing an IllegalArgumentException instead of a ClassCastException when trying to add a non-comparable value to the frequency table. The fix involves updating the method to throw the correct exception.

patches.hypothesis: The method was not handling null values correctly, causing the IllegalArgumentException to be thrown instead of the expected ClassCastException when adding a non-comparable object to the frequency table.
patches.changes:
+ Null check for the input parameter
- Changed the ClassCastException to be thrown instead of IllegalArgumentException in case of non-comparable input
patches.diff:
+ Null check for the input parameter
- Changed the ClassCastException to be thrown instead of IllegalArgumentException in case of non-comparable input

patches.hypothesis: The bug is caused by the method throwing an IllegalArgumentException instead of ClassCastException when a non-comparable value is added to the frequency table. The fix involves changing the exception thrown to ClassCastException in the tree map.
The test case is expecting a ClassCastException, but the method is throwing an IllegalArgumentException instead.
patches.changes:
- throw new IllegalArgumentException("Value not comparable to existing values.");
+ throw new ClassCastException("Value not comparable to existing values.");
patches.diff:
- throw new IllegalArgumentException("Value not comparable to existing values.");
+ throw new ClassCastException("Value not comparable to existing values.");
